text: The surgeon takes down small bowel and omental adhesions to the colostomy. The rectal stump is identified and mobilized. The surgeon works laterally to mobilize the descending colon up to the splenic flexure, preparing to take down the colostomy for further mobilization.

The surgeon clears the ostomy from the fascia and mobilizes the splenic flexure, encountering significant scarring. The port placement includes a 12mm stapler port in the right lower quadrant, a camera port in the right peri-umbilical area, and two 8mm ports. The surgeon clears the mesentery from the colon, preparing to staple across it.

The surgeon uses a tip-up grasper to prevent the anvil from falling too far into the colon while an assistant uses a Kelly clamp to slide the anvil into the colostomy. A blue load stapler is used to transect the colon at the level of the fascia. The surgeon avoids using the plastic spike on the anvil, opting instead to make a collotomy at the staple line in the middle of the descending colon, allowing the anvil to be pushed through easily. The procedure involves entering through the gastrocolic ligament into the lesser sac to mobilize the splenic flexure, addressing significant scarring of the omentum to the spleen.

The surgeon completes the mobilization of the splenic flexure and proceeds to medialize the colon for additional length. The rectal stump is exposed, revealing a mobile segment of the sigmoid colon, facilitating an easy resection. The surgeon trims the top of the rectal stump due to scarring, ensuring compliance for stapler passage. A technique is demonstrated where the additional specimen is sewn to the colostomy for removal. The posterior rectus sheath is closed robotically, followed by the closure of the anterior fascia after colostomy removal.

The surgeon performs an end-to-end anastomosis using a 29 EEA stapler, passing rectal sizers from 25 to 33 to accommodate the anvil. A rigid proctoscope is used to conduct a leak test, confirming the integrity of the anastomosis. The robot is undocked, and the colostomy is excised, followed by closure of the anterior fascia.
